_meta.description: Per-domain empirical calibration records for Elara RRG proxy. Each entry is a reusable template — copy params into proxy.py / calibrate_proxy.py for the matching use case.

fields.label: Human-readable description
fields.MIN_KEEP_PAIRS: Minimum verbatim pairs kept after compression
fields.sessions: Number of sessions used for calibration

entries.label: Coding Q&A — conversational (Python, algorithms, async, descriptors)
entries.method: calibrate_proxy.py --collect --domain coding --W 3 --n-per-cat 6 (18 sessions total, 3 categories × 6 each)
entries.notes: Covers conversational coding Q&A (questions about Python concepts, algorithms, async, descriptors). tau_lock_dr is 24% higher than PROXY-01 prose (0.027 vs 0.022) — coding sessions have more consistent technical vocabulary, raising baseline d_rho. VARIED has perfect separation (p01=0.058 >> tau=0.027). MIXED lock_frac=0.50 throughout sweep — coding MIXED templates are too semantically close to COHERENT; the model treats them as related. Gap: does not cover code-paste patterns (user pastes raw code for fix/review). For code-paste calibration, add CODING-02 with templates where user messages contain large code blocks.

entries.label: Naturalistic prose — deepening Q&A (Spanish/English)
entries.notes: Validated end-to-end: COHERENT lock_frac=0.750 (43.9% simulated token savings), MIXED=0.250, VARIED=0.500. Gate 1 fires once per session at turn W (d_rho=0 by Var([single])); subsequent turns see d_rho>>tau_lock_dr as rho_ab accumulates variance. MIN_KEEP_PAIRS lowered 3→2 on 2026-05-07 to allow cut at first eligible turn (turn 4, 7 messages).
